title: Feature Request: Multi-page Extra Keys with Horizontal Swipe
author: hastagaming
body:
### Feature description

## Summary

I would like to request support for multiple pages in `extra-keys`, allowing users to switch between pages by swiping horizontally (similar to Gboard symbol pages).

Currently, `extra-keys` only supports one layout (one or two rows). Users who need many keys must either make buttons very small or frequently edit `termux.properties`.

## Proposed Configuration

Example:

```properties
extra-keys = [
  [
    ["ESC","KEYBOARD","HOME","UP","END","PGUP"],
    ["TAB","DRAWER","CTRL","ALT","LEFT","DOWN","RIGHT","PGDN"]
  ],
  [
    ["|","-","_","/","=","BKSP","DEL","FN"],
    ["&",";",":","\"","'","(",")","`"]
  ],
  [
    ["F1","F2","F3","F4","F5","F6"],
    ["F7","F8","F9","F10","F11","F12"]
  ]
]
```
Each top-level array would represent one page.

## UI Proposal
- Horizontal swipe to switch pages.
- Optional page indicators (dots).
- Smooth animation.
- Backward compatible with existing extra-keys configurations.

## Benefits
- More shortcuts without reducing button size.
- Better experience for programming and terminal workflows.
- No breaking changes for existing users.

### Additional information

### Environment

- Termux version: 0.119.0-beta.3
- Installation source: F-Droid
- Android version: 15
- Device: realme RMX3834

### Current behavior

`extra-keys` only supports a single layout (one or two rows). Attempting to define multiple pages results in a configuration parsing error, and there is currently no way to organize a large number of extra keys into separate swipeable pages.

### Expected behavior

Allow multiple pages in `extra-keys`, where each top-level array represents one page. Users should be able to switch pages by swiping horizontally.

Example:

```properties
extra-keys = [
  [
    ["ESC","KEYBOARD","HOME","UP","END","PGUP"],
    ["TAB","DRAWER","CTRL","ALT","LEFT","DOWN","RIGHT","PGDN"]
  ],
  [
    ["|","-","_","/","=","BKSP","DEL","FN"],
    ["&",";",":","\"","'","(",")","`"]
  ]
]
```

## Use case
This would make it much easier to access navigation keys, programming symbols, Git shortcuts, function keys, and other frequently used keys without shrinking button sizes or manually editing the configuration.
The interaction could be similar to the symbol pages in Gboard, using horizontal swipe gestures to switch between pages while keeping existing configurations fully backward compatible.

title: [Bug]: Firefox on Termux X11 native freezes during Firefox Sync OAuth (oauth_webchannel_v1)
author: Yuki-Soul
body:
### Problem description

Firefox native installed from the Termux x11 repository cannot sign into Firefox Sync.
The issue is NOT specific to Passkeys.
Both password login and Passkey login fail when initiated from:
about:preferences#sync
or
accounts.firefox.com/signin?context=oauth_webchannel_v1...
Normal login to accounts.firefox.com works correctly.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior.

1. pkg install firefox
2. Start Firefox.
3. Open about:preferences#sync
4. Click Sign in.
5. Try:
   - Password login
   - Passkey login
6. Result:
    Login with Password:
        - Sign In button becomes disabled (greyed out)
        - Nothing happens afterwards
    Login with Passkey:
        - Browser freezes completely
        - Browser must be force killed

### What is the expected behavior?

Firefox should complete OAuth and connect to Firefox Sync.

### System information

* Termux application version:  TERMUX_VERSION=0.118.3
* Android OS version:  Android 16

title: [Bug]: Bootstrap install fails or leaves a blank terminal when the activity is recreated on first launch
author: briqt
body:
## Problem description

On a fresh install, the first launch of Termux can either:

- fail with **"Unable to install bootstrap"**, or
- finish installing the bootstrap but leave a **blank terminal with no session created**.

Tapping "Try again" / relaunching usually recovers, which is what makes it look intermittent.

### Root cause

Bootstrap installation is started from `TermuxActivity.onServiceConnected()`, and the `whenDone` callback that creates the first terminal session is bound to that specific activity instance. If the activity is **recreated while the install thread is still running**, two things go wrong:

1. The recreated activity re-enters `TermuxInstaller.setupBootstrapIfNeeded()` and starts a **second install thread** that races with the first one on the same `$STAGING`/`$PREFIX` directory. Deleting the staging directory then fails with `DirectoryNotEmptyException` → the "Unable to install bootstrap" dialog.
2. The original install thread finishes against the now-destroyed activity, whose `whenDone` bails out on `mTermuxService == null`, so **no session is ever created** and the terminal stays blank.

An activity recreation on first launch is easy to hit in practice. On MIUI/HyperOS the system resets a `targetSdk`-28 app's compat-mode configuration right after install and relaunches the activity, so it reproduces on **every** fresh install there. Other ROMs hit it less often, but the race is latent everywhere.

### Relevant logcat (fresh install, MIUI/HyperOS)

```
TermuxInstaller: Installing Termux bootstrap packages.
TermuxInstaller: Extracting bootstrap zip to prefix staging directory ".../files/usr-staging".
MiuiCompatModePackages: package com.termux updated, removing config
ActivityThread: Schedule relaunch activity: com.termux.app.TermuxActivity
WindowManager: android.view.WindowLeaked: ... at TermuxInstaller.setupBootstrapIfNeeded(TermuxInstaller.java) at TermuxActivity.onServiceConnected(TermuxActivity.java)
TermuxInstaller: Installing Termux bootstrap packages.            <-- 2nd install thread (recreated activity)
TermuxInstaller: Bootstrap Error: ... Deleting termux prefix staging directory ... DirectoryNotEmptyException
TermuxInstaller: Bootstrap packages installed successfully.      <-- 1st thread finishes, but no session is created -> blank terminal
```

## Steps to reproduce the behavior.

1. Build a debug APK from current `master`.
2. On a device that recreates the activity on first launch (reliably reproducible on MIUI/HyperOS, where a `targetSdk`-28 app is relaunched right after install), uninstall any previous Termux, then install the APK.
3. Launch Termux for the first time.

Result: either the "Unable to install bootstrap" dialog, or a blank terminal with no session. Reproduces on every uninstall/reinstall cycle on the device below.

## What is the expected behavior?

The bootstrap installs exactly once and the first session is created, regardless of whether the activity is recreated during the install.

## Proposed fix (verified)

Both problems can be fixed in `TermuxInstaller`, without trying to prevent the (system-driven) recreation:

- Guard the install with an `AtomicBoolean` so only **one** install thread runs at a time; a concurrent caller from the recreated activity returns instead of racing on the files.
- Keep the most recent `whenDone` callback in a `static` field; the single install thread runs it on completion, so the **currently-alive** activity creates the first session no matter how many times the activity was recreated during the install.

Patch is branched off current `master`, single file, `+31/-1`:

- Diff vs master: https://github.com/termux/termux-app/compare/master...briqt:termux-app:fix-bootstrap-install-activity-recreate
- Commit: https://github.com/briqt/termux-app/commit/f063d99d

(I would have opened a PR, but this repo currently limits opening PRs to collaborators, so I'm filing this issue instead — please feel free to cherry-pick.)

Verified on the device below across many uninstall/reinstall cycles: the activity is still recreated once during install, but it is now fully tolerated — no error, and the session is created every time.

## System information

* Termux application version: 0.118.0 (debug build from current `master`)
* Android OS version: Android 14 (HyperOS 2.0.6.0)
* Device model: Xiaomi Mi 11 (M2011K2C)

title: [Feature]: Consolidate standalone plugin APKs into monolithic CLI packages (`termux-plugin-*`) to eliminate `sharedUserId`
author: SjnExe
body:
### Feature description

This proposal suggests migrating the background execution logic of all optional Termux plugins (`Termux:API`, `Termux:Boot`, `Termux:Float`, `Termux:Styling`, `Termux:Tasker`, `Termux:Widget`) directly into the core `termux-app` codebase. 

Instead of separate APK installations, the user-facing command-line utilities would be distributed cleanly as standard modular packages (`termux-plugin-api`, `termux-plugin-styling`, etc.) via the `apt`/`pkg` package manager. The main application would act as a single, centralized permission router and IPC hub.

### Core Architecture & Structural Motivation
The current multi-APK plugin system relies heavily on `android:sharedUserId` to allow separate applications to access Termux’s private filesystem (`/data/data/com.termux`). 

Because Google has deprecated `sharedUserId` due to non-deterministic package manager behaviors, and because it causes severe cryptographic signature mismatches for users switching between F-Droid, GitHub Actions, and custom compilation pipelines, eliminating it is critical. Merging the plugin handling into the core app permanently cuts this dependency.

Instead of sending Android Intent broadcasts across separate applications, the package-installed CLI tools would communicate with the parent app's background services using local filesystem Unix Domain Sockets mapped inside the application's secure `$TMPDIR` or internal state directory.

### Internal Privilege Separation & Security Paradigm
Since a monolithic Termux app must declare powerful hardware permissions (Camera, Location, Biometrics) in its global `AndroidManifest.xml`, any script running inside the terminal would inherently share the app's single Android UID and access those capabilities. To prevent malicious or accidental API abuse, we propose an internal access-control layer modeled after Linux capabilities:

1. **Calling-Process Verification:** When a CLI wrapper (such as `termux-camera-photo`) executes, the parent app's background service checks the calling process credentials using `/proc/[pid]/exe` to confirm the binary hasn't been substituted or tampered with.
2. **Virtual Capability Mapping:** Maintain an internal ACL (Access Control List) ledger within the main app that flags which binaries are allowed to communicate with specific Android API subsystems.
3. **Runtime Authorization Prompts:** For highly sensitive interactions (e.g., location tracking, file injections, biometric loops), Termux can intercept the internal socket request and display a command-line prompt, a native Android biometric overlay, or a device credential check before executing the API call.
4. **Command-Line Audit Utility:** Introduce a tool (e.g., `termux-privs`) to inspect and configure permissions directly from the terminal shell:
   ```bash
   # Check which internal plugins are authorized
   termux-privs status
   
   # Revoke a specific utility pathway's hardware access
   termux-privs revoke api-location

### Modernized Storage Access (`Android/data` & `Android obb`)
Consolidating the package architecture offers a clean hook to resolve scoped storage limitations imposed on Android 11 and higher. Because standard terminal environments are blocked by the OS from directly reading app-specific storage paths via `MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, Termux can embed:

*   **Native SAFD ocument Tree Parser:** An integrated Storage Access Framework loop within the main application layer to handle abstract file URIs contextually.
*   **Shizuku/Sui Integration Client:** A built-in binder interface that allows users to leverage system-level shell privileges to mount or bridge `/sdcard/Android/data/` and `/sdcard/Android obb/` pathways directly into the environment without requiring device root privileges.

---

> *This feature request was researched, validated for Android/Linux operating system constraints, and formatted with the assistance of an AI collaborator to evaluate Linux capability models, Android runtime IPC frameworks, and current `sharedUserId` architectural pain-points within the Termux ecosystem.*

### Additional information

### Relevant Background / Links
This architectural change is directly aligned with ongoing long-term discussions among Termux maintainers:

* **Termux Issue #2654 (Plugins without shared UID):** Maintainers have previously noted to the community that certain lightweight plugins (like `termux-boot` and `termux-styling`) are already primary candidates to be directly merged into the core `termux-app` codebase to eliminate installation and signature conflicts.
* **Termux Discussion #4000 (PlayStore App Updates Announcement):** Outlines the frozen user experience caused by signature mismatches across deployment platforms (F-Droid vs Play Store vs GitHub) precisely due to `sharedUserId` locks.
* **Upstream Parallels:** Traditional Linux package managers (like Debian's `apt`) natively treat supplemental software wrappers (like `git-email`, `git-gui`) as modular binary split-packages rather than standalone virtualization bundles, maintaining a unified platform permission layout.

**Links:**
* https://github.com/termux/termux-app/issues/2654
* https://github.com/termux/termux-app/discussions/4000

title: [Bug]: Termux can't connect to LAN on Android 17
author: joc3j
body:
### Problem description

Due to changes introduced in Android 17 a separate permission is necessary for apps to access LAN

For further information see
https://developer.android.com/privacy-and-security/local-network-permission

### Steps to reproduce the behavior.

1. Install Android 17 (for example on a Pixel device with GrapheneOS build 2026062800)
2. Install Termux
3. Try to SSH to a server on LAN while connected to Wi-Fi network
4. Connection fails

### What is the expected behavior?

Termux needs to declare ACCESS_LOCAL_NETWORK permission in it's manifest after which user will be prompted to allow access to LAN

### System information

* Termux application version: 2026.06.21 Google Play
* Android OS version: 17, GrapheneOS
* Device model: Pixel 9

title: [Bug]: termux-open cannot open any file on Android 16 (vivo/Funtouch OS)
author: Tekno125
body:
### Problem description

# `termux-open` cannot open any file on Android 16 (vivo/Funtouch OS)

## Description

`termux-open` can successfully open URLs, but it fails to open **any local file**.

Every application receiving the file reports that it cannot find or access it.

Examples:

- Google Chrome: **"File not found"** or **"Your file could not be accessed"**
- Acode: file not found
- vivo Doc Master: file not found
- HTML Viewer: file not found

However, the files definitely exist and are readable from Termux.

## termux-info

TERMUX_APK_RELEASE=GITHUB                             TERMUX_APP_PACKAGE_MANAGER=apt
TERMUX_APP_PID=31528                                  TERMUX_APP__DATA_DIR=/data/user/0/com.termux
TERMUX_APP__LEGACY_DATA_DIR=/data/data/com.termux
TERMUX_APP__SE_FILE_CONTEXT=u:object_r:app_data_file:s0:c39,c257,c512,c768
TERMUX_APP__SE_INFO=default:targetSdkVersion=28:complete
TERMUX_IS_DEBUGGABLE_BUILD=1
TERMUX_MAIN_PACKAGE_FORMAT=debian                     TERMUX_VERSION=0.118.3                                TERMUX__HOME=/data/data/com.termux/files/home
TERMUX__PREFIX=/data/data/com.termux/files/usr
TERMUX__ROOTFS_DIR=/data/data/com.termux/files
TERMUX__SE_PROCESS_CONTEXT=u:r:untrusted_app_27:s0:c39,c257,c512,c768
TERMUX__USER_ID=0
Packages CPU architecture:
aarch64
Subscribed repositories:
# sources.list
deb https://packages-cf.termux.dev/apt/termux-main stable main                                              Updatable packages:
command-not-found/stable 3.4.1-16 aarch64 [upgradable from: 3.4.1-15]
libngtcp2/stable 1.24.0 aarch64 [upgradable from: 1.23.0]
npm/stable 11.18.0 all [upgradable from: 11.17.0]
termux-tools version:
1.45.0
Android version:
16
Kernel build information:
Linux localhost 5.15.178-android13-8-00007-g362d545d31a5-ab14608873 #1 SMP PREEMPT Wed Dec 17 16:29:33 UTC 2025 aarch64 Android
Device manufacturer:
vivo
Device model:
V2556
Supported ABIs:
SUPPORTED_ABIS: arm64-v8a,armeabi-v7a,armeabi
SUPPORTED_32_BIT_ABIS: armeabi-v7a,armeabi
SUPPORTED_64_BIT_ABIS: arm64-v8a
LD Variables:
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=
LD_PRELOAD=/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/lib/libtermux-exec-ld-preload.so

## Notes

I have reproduced this with multiple file types:

- `.txt`
- `.html`

All produce the same result.

The issue only affects opening local files through `termux-open`. Opening URLs works normally.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior.

## Steps to reproduce

Create a simple file:

```sh
echo "hello" > ~/test.txt
```

Verify that it exists:

```sh
ls -l ~/test.txt
```

Open it:

```sh
termux-open ~/test.txt
```

Or:

```sh
termux-open --chooser ~/test.txt
```


### What is the expected behavior?

## Expected behavior

The selected application should receive and open the file.

## Actual behavior

The target application opens but reports that the file cannot be found or accessed.

## Additional testing

Storage permission is granted and working correctly.

`termux-setup-storage` completed successfully.

Shared storage is accessible:

```sh
cd ~/storage/shared
ls
```

works normally.

Files inside shared storage also fail:

```sh
termux-open ~/storage/shared/Documents/example.html
```

Applications still report that the file cannot be found.

URLs work correctly:

```sh
termux-open-url https://example.com
```

This opens Chrome without any problem.

Launching a file using `am` with a `file://` URI works differently:

```sh
am start \
-a android.intent.action.VIEW \
-d "file:///storage/emulated/0/Documents/example.html" \
-t "text/html"
```

Applications capable of opening `file://` URIs can access the file successfully.

This suggests the problem may be specific to the `content://` URI used by `termux-open`.

### System information

- **Termux version:** 0.118.3 (GitHub release)
- **termux-tools:** 1.45.0
- **Android:** 16 (official stable, never joined beta)
- **Device:** vivo V2556
- **Architecture:** aarch64

title: [Feature]: 16KB Support
author: kholifa-network
body:
### Feature description

Android 15 introduces support for 16 KB page sizes, and Google Play will mandate that all new apps and app updates targeting Android 15+ must be compatible with 16 KB devices starting November 2025.

Currently, the native library libtermux.so produced by the terminal-emulator module is built without the required linker flag, resulting in the following build warning:

"APK is not compatible with 16 KB devices. Some libraries have LOAD segments not aligned at 16 KB boundaries: lib/arm64-v8a/[libtermux.so](https://libtermux.so/)"

This prevents apps that depend on the Termux terminal library from being published on Google Play when targeting Android 15+.
Proposed solution

Add the linker flag -Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384 to the NDK build configuration. In the terminal-emulator/build.gradle file, inside the externalNativeBuild block, the arguments can be updated as follows:
groovy

externalNativeBuild {
    ndkBuild {
        arguments "NDK_APP_LDFLAGS=-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384"
        // ... other arguments
    }
}

This change ensures that the native library is compiled with 16 KB ELF alignment, making it compatible with 16 KB page size devices while remaining backward compatible.

I have tested this fix locally by building the terminal-emulator module with the flag, and it resolves the warning without any functional issues. It would be great if the Termux library could include this flag in its official builds (both GitHub releases and JitPack) so that all downstream apps can benefit.

### Additional information

Android 16 KB page size documentation: https://developer.android.com/guide/practices/page-sizes

Google Play 16 KB support requirement: https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/113469#16kb

Relevant code file: terminal-emulator/build.gradle (the externalNativeBuild.ndkBuild section)

Termux release that still produces the warning: v0.118.3 (stable) and v0.119.0-beta.3

title: [Bug]: There are potential time-consuming operations on the UI thread.
author: ab13431
body:
### Problem description

We used a static analysis tool to check whether termux-app performs any potentially time-consuming operations on the UI thread. Our analysis identified the following potential issues:

In RunCommandService.java, within the onStartCommand() method, the operation involves file I/O or SharedPreferences access, which can trigger disk reads or writes synchronously on the main thread. According to Android best practices, such operations should be moved off the UI thread and executed in a background thread or coroutine. A test case was generated (`RunCommandService.onStartCommand.kt`) but could not be fully compiled and verified in the sandbox environment — manual review is recommended.

We hope this report helps improve responsiveness and user experience.

### What is the expected behavior?

title: [Feature]: Send android.intent.extra.SUBJECT as the "$2" parameter when sharing an URL with Termux (termux-url-opener)
author: rodrigo-morales-1
body: ### Feature description Currently, Termux only sends `android.intent.extra.TEXT` to `~/bin/termux-url-opener` as the parameter `$1`. I believe it would be convenient for some users if Termux also sended `android.intent.extra.SUBJECT` as the parameter `$2`. ### Additional information ## A dummy script to see how Termux send parameters I confirmed this behavior by writing the following script. ```sh cat /data/data/com.termux/files/home/bin/termux-url-opener ``` ``` #!/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/bash url="$1" echo >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt date >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt echo "1: $1" >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt echo "2: $2" >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt echo "3: $3" >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt echo "4: $4" >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt echo "@: $@" >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt echo "*: $*" >> /sdcard/Download/test.txt ``` I opened a random URL (e.g. [Demo Background Sample Video - Youtube](https://youtube.com/watch?v=K4TOrB7at0Y)) in my web browser. Then, I pressed the button "Share", then I selected Termux as a Share target. The script termux-url-opener was executed, so the file `/sdcard/Download/test.txt` changed to: ```sh cat /sdcard/Download/test.txt ``` ``` Tue Jun 9 18:39:45 -05 2026 1: https://youtube.com/watch?v=K4TOrB7at0Y 2: 3: 4: @: https://youtube.com/watch?v=K4TOrB7at0Y *: https://youtube.com/watch?v=K4TOrB7at0Y ``` ## The reason why I want to get `android.text.intent.SUBJECT` I use the app [Tasks.org](https://f-droid.org/es/packages/org.tasks/) to manage TODO tasks. When that app is installed, it is added as a "Share" target. When sharing a website through Firefox, the title of the website is added as the title of the TODO task and the URL is added as the description of the TODO task. I want to accomplish something similar in Termux: I want to make termux-url-opener append the URL and the title of the website to a file called `read-later.txt`. The screenshot below shows the window Tasks.org after pressing "Share" from Fitrfox and selecting "Tasks.org" in the "Share" menu. ## Behavior when using "Share" from Firefox and Youtube Since I'm not familiar with how Android apps send parameters under the hood through the "Share" button, I decided to look into that. I downloaded the app [Intent Intercept](https://f-droid.org/packages/de.k3b.android.intentintercept). Then I visited a random website (e.g. [Demo Background Sample Video - Youtube](https://youtube.com/watch?v=K4TOrB7at0Y)), I pressed the button "Share" from the apps Firefox and Youtube and then I seleced "Intent Intercept" in the menu opened after pressing "Share". The screenshots below show the app "Intent Intercept" after pressing "Share" in those apps. The code block contains the confent of the field "URI". Note that in the "URI", the title of the website is stored at `android.intent.extra.SUBJECT`, while the URL of the website is stored at `android.intent.extra.TEXT`. **From Firefox:** URI: ```text intent:#Intent;action=android.intent.action.SEND;type=text/plain;launchFlags=0x18080000;S.android.intent.extra.SUBJECT=Demo%20Background%20Sample%20Video%20-%20YouTube;S.android.intent.extra.TEXT=https%3A%2F%2Fm.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DK4TOrB7at0Y;end ``` Screenshot: **From Youtube** URI: ```text intent:#Intent;action=android.intent.action.SEND;type=text/plain;launchFlags=0x1b080000;launchHwFlags=0x400;S.android.intent.extra.SUBJECT=Demo%20Background%20Sample%20Video;S.android.intent.extra.TEXT=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DK4TOrB7at0Y%26si%3D5oAhlUNaMdtr6qnR;end ``` Screenshot: As I mentioned, I'm not familiar with how Android apps share paramters through the "Share" button, but it seems that `android.intent.extra.SUBJECT` is commonly used for storing the title of a website or a video, so passing as `$2` to `~/bin/termux-url-opener` would be convenient for Termux users that use `~/bin/termux-url-opener`. ## Current workaround I'm currently using `curl` to send a request, and then using a HTML parser to get the title of a website. I wish I didn't have to send that second request because it sometimes take 2-3 seconds to complete and those few seconds do have a significant impact in my daily routine. ``` $ cat /data/data/com.termux/files/home/bin/termux-url-opener ``` ```sh #!/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/bash url="$1" url_encoded="$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('''$url'''))")" title="$(curl --silent --location --header 'Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.9' --header 'Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8' --header 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:151.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/151.0' "$url" | pup 'head title text{}' | head -n 1)" title_encoded="$(python3 -c "import urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.quote('''$title'''))")" emacsclient --socket-name my-emacs-daemon "org-protocol://capture?template=my-template-1&url=$url_encoded&title=$title_encoded" ```
